GOOD SAMARITAN SOCIETY NEW UNDERWOOD

CCN: 435104 · NEW UNDERWOOD, SD 57761 · Pennington County

Overview

Address
412 SOUTH MADISON, NEW UNDERWOOD, SD 57761
Phone
6057546489
Certified beds
41
Avg daily residents
36 (88% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1997-07-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.05 3.26 3.62 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.77 0.57 0.91 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.67 0.72 0.80 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.61 1.96 1.91 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.44 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.10 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.52
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.38
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.99
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
69.0%
annual
RN turnover
55.6%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.15
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.84
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-08 12 10 2 88 1 88
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2025-01-30 12 12 0 116 1 116
Total weighted health score
95.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (29)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

27 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-29
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-12-08 2026-01-29
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-29
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-16
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2025-12-08 2026-01-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-28
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-12-08 2026-01-23
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. D 2025-12-08 2026-01-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-12-08 2026-01-29
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-07-09 2025-07-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-06-10 2025-06-30
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. F 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2025-01-30 2025-02-27
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-11-16 2023-12-15
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-11-16 2023-12-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-11-16 2023-12-15
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2023-11-16 2023-12-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2023-11-16 2023-12-15
